zone.label: South India
zone.insight: South India is now the clearest savory cluster: Gunpowder Podi leads across Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Telangana, with Nippattu Spice and Curry Leaf reinforcing the roasted-spice profile in Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. Sweet reads are strong but more state-specific, led by Kaju Katli, Mysore Pak, Kesar Milk, and Qubani Apricot.
zone.keyFlavors: Gunpowder Podi, Nippattu Spice, Curry Leaf, Kaju Katli, Mysore Pak, Kesar Milk, Qubani Apricot
zone.signal: High — lead with savory regional spice platforms, then layer market-specific premium sweet variants.

zone.label: North India
zone.insight: Delhi NCR, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, and Haryana remain the strongest mithai-to-snack conversion markets. Ras Malai, Rabri, Phirni, Gulab Jamun, Kaju Katli, and Kesar Milk shape the sweet opportunity, while Chaat Masala anchors Delhi NCR, Haryana, and Uttar Pradesh and Tandoori/Achari cues support Punjab and broader North savory demand.
zone.keyFlavors: Ras Malai, Rabri, Phirni, Gulab Jamun, Kesar Milk, Chaat Masala, Tandoori Spice, Achari Spice
zone.signal: High — fusion snack formats can borrow directly from mithai and chaat occasions.

zone.label: West India
zone.insight: West India has shifted to a state-specific texture, dairy-sweet, coastal sour, and heat read. Gujarat brings Shrikhand, Methi Masala, Sev Masala, and Green Chilli Lime; Maharashtra adds Kaju Katli, Modak Coconut, Garlic Chilli, Schezwan, and Kokum; Goa contributes Coconut Jaggery, Bebinca Caramel, Recheado Spice, and Kokum; Rajasthan adds Ghevar and Red Chilli.
zone.keyFlavors: Shrikhand, Kaju Katli, Modak Coconut, Coconut Jaggery, Bebinca Caramel, Ghevar, Methi Masala, Recheado Spice, Kokum, Red Chilli
zone.signal: High — use market-specific flavor architecture rather than a single West India proposition.

zone.label: East India
zone.insight: East India continues to index toward layered regional sweets and heritage savories rather than blunt heat. West Bengal centers on Nolen Gur, Mishti Doi, Kasundi, Jhalmuri Masala, and Panch Phoron; Odisha adds Rasgulla, Chhena Poda, Ras Malai, and Panch Phoron; Bihar and Jharkhand bring Soan Papdi, Til Jaggery, Sattu Masala, Mustard, and Garlic Chilli.
zone.keyFlavors: Nolen Gur, Mishti Doi, Rasgulla, Chhena Poda, Soan Papdi, Til Jaggery, Kasundi, Jhalmuri Masala, Panch Phoron, Sattu Masala
zone.signal: Moderate to high — quality, provenance, and regional authenticity matter more than novelty.

zone.label: Northeast India
zone.insight: The Northeast remains the strongest discovery zone, built around forest, fruit, ferment, and fire cues. Wild Honey, Passion Fruit, Pineapple, Black Rice Sweet, and Black Sesame lead sweet discovery, while Bhut Jolokia, Smoked Chilli, Bamboo Shoot, Turmeric, Bird's Eye Chilli, Sesame Chilli, and Mosdeng Chilli create a savory territory with little overlap elsewhere in India.
zone.keyFlavors: Wild Honey, Passion Fruit, Pineapple, Black Rice Sweet, Bhut Jolokia, Smoked Chilli, Bamboo Shoot, Turmeric, Mosdeng Chilli
zone.signal: Low volume but high distinctiveness — best suited to limited drops, creator-led discovery, and local collaborations.

zone.label: Central India
zone.insight: Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh show a practical bridge between comfort sweets and street-snack spice. Jalebi, Rabri, Gajar Halwa, Coconut Jaggery, and Til Jaggery support sweet familiarity, while Namkeen Masala, Chana Jor Masala, Garlic Chilli, Chaat Masala, Hing Jeera, Tamarind, and Red Chilli point to strong regional-snack white space.
zone.keyFlavors: Jalebi, Rabri, Gajar Halwa, Coconut Jaggery, Til Jaggery, Namkeen Masala, Chana Jor Masala, Garlic Chilli, Hing Jeera
zone.signal: High white space — translate regional street-snack cues into accessible crackers, puffs, and spiced biscuits.

weather.label: Winter (Oct–Feb)
weather.insight: Winter drives sweet indulgence nationally. Gajar Halwa, Rabri, Nolen Gur, Til Jaggery, Coconut Jaggery, and Kesar Milk spike as warm, rich, comfort-led profiles and carry strong celebration associations.
weather.peakFlavors: Gajar Halwa, Rabri, Nolen Gur, Til Jaggery, Coconut Jaggery, Kesar Milk
weather.implication: Sweet snack innovation should be ready before October, with the strongest window running through February.

weather.label: Summer (Mar–Jun)
weather.insight: Summer lifts cooling, tangy, and lighter flavor systems. Pudina Masala, Kokum, Green Chilli Lime, Tamarind, Gondhoraj Lime, Shrikhand, and fruit-led cues such as Passion Fruit and Pineapple become more relevant as consumers look for refreshing snack profiles.
weather.peakFlavors: Pudina Masala, Kokum, Green Chilli Lime, Tamarind, Gondhoraj Lime, Shrikhand, Passion Fruit, Pineapple
weather.implication: Credible cooling or tangy notes give packaged snacks a clearer seasonal role in March-June.

weather.label: Monsoon (Jul–Sep)
weather.insight: Monsoon lifts fried, hot, crispy, and street-adjacent snack formats. Jalebi, Ghevar, Chaat Masala, Sev Masala, Namkeen Masala, Garlic Chilli, and Tandoori Spice map naturally to the chai-snack ritual.
weather.peakFlavors: Jalebi, Ghevar, Chaat Masala, Sev Masala, Namkeen Masala, Garlic Chilli, Tandoori Spice
weather.implication: Savory launches and chai-time bundles have the clearest monsoon fit, especially for crackers and puffed snacks.

weather.label: Festive (Sep–Nov)
weather.insight: Festive season compresses a large share of sweet growth into the Navratri-Diwali window. Kaju Katli, Motichoor, Mysore Pak, Soan Papdi, Gulab Jamun, Rabri, Phirni, and Ghevar all benefit from gifting and celebration occasions.
weather.peakFlavors: Kaju Katli, Motichoor, Mysore Pak, Soan Papdi, Gulab Jamun, Rabri, Phirni, Ghevar
weather.implication: Any sweet-led launch should be on shelf before September to capture the peak festive demand window.

weather.label: Year-round savory
weather.insight: Core savory flavors are less seasonally constrained than sweets. Chaat Masala, Gunpowder Podi, Garlic Chilli, Tamarind, Curry Leaf, Black Pepper, and Red Chilli remain useful planning anchors across months.
weather.peakFlavors: Chaat Masala, Gunpowder Podi, Garlic Chilli, Tamarind, Curry Leaf, Black Pepper, Red Chilli
weather.implication: Savory launches carry lower timing risk and make better first-entry SKUs in new state markets.

age.label: Gen Z discovery
age.insight: Gen Z is driving the Northeast flavor discovery wave. Wild Honey, Passion Fruit, Smoked Chilli, Black Rice Sweet, Bamboo Shoot and Bhut Jolokia are disproportionately suited to younger discovery behavior because they travel through food creators and short-form content before mass retail.
age.topFlavors: Wild Honey, Passion Fruit, Smoked Chilli, Black Rice Sweet, Bamboo Shoot, Bhut Jolokia
age.trial: Very high — seed through creator-led digital before scaling into broader retail.

age.label: Millennial crossover
age.insight: Millennials own the mithai-snack crossover. Nolen Gur, Ras Malai, Rabri, Phirni, Qubani Apricot, Kaju Katli, and Gondhoraj Lime carry nostalgia but fit into portion-controlled biscuit, wafer, bar, and cake formats for daily consumption.
age.topFlavors: Nolen Gur, Ras Malai, Rabri, Phirni, Qubani Apricot, Kaju Katli, Gondhoraj Lime
age.trial: High — strongest when familiar regional flavors are modernized into repeatable snack occasions.

age.label: Mass consumers (35+)
age.insight: Mass consumers 35 and above anchor established flavors. Chaat Masala, Gulab Jamun, Kaju Katli, Coconut Jaggery, Jalebi, Kesar Milk, and Rabri are lower-risk, high-familiarity bets whose growth is driven more by category expansion than novelty seeking.
age.topFlavors: Chaat Masala, Gulab Jamun, Kaju Katli, Coconut Jaggery, Jalebi, Kesar Milk, Rabri
age.trial: Moderate — stronger for familiar extensions and family-safe formats than experimental drops.

age.label: Health-conscious
age.insight: Health-conscious urban consumers cluster around functional regional flavors. Sattu Masala, Til Jaggery, Turmeric, Bhang Jeera, Methi Masala, Black Pepper, and Wild Honey work because they feel familiar first and functional second.
age.topFlavors: Sattu Masala, Til Jaggery, Turmeric, Bhang Jeera, Methi Masala, Black Pepper, Wild Honey
age.trial: High — NutriChoice-style products can own this space without making the proposition feel medicinal.

age.label: Urban youth heat
age.insight: Urban youth continue to drive Indo-fusion heat. Schezwan, Gunpowder Podi, Gongura, Garlic Chilli, Red Chilli, Bird's Eye Chilli, and Mosdeng Chilli are chosen for boldness and perceived edge, and they respond better to limited editions, collaborations, and social-first launches than to retail-led distribution alone.
age.topFlavors: Schezwan, Gunpowder Podi, Gongura, Garlic Chilli, Red Chilli, Bird's Eye Chilli, Mosdeng Chilli
age.trial: Very high — use limited editions, collabs, and social launches to keep the cue fresh.

age.label: Premium gifting
age.insight: Premium gifting consumers elevate regional sweets. Mysore Pak, Nolen Gur, Bebinca Caramel, Bal Mithai, Kaju Katli, Ghevar, and Qubani Apricot are gaining as SEC A consumers shift gifting spend from generic national mithai to artisan regional formats.
age.topFlavors: Mysore Pak, Nolen Gur, Bebinca Caramel, Bal Mithai, Kaju Katli, Ghevar, Qubani Apricot
age.trial: High-value niche — a strong entry point for premium biscuit formats and gift packaging.
